layout.title: Open-Q

step_welcome.what: Read the research objectives and the specific instructions for participation.
step_welcome.why: Beyond standard ethical consent, this ensures you grasp the specific 'condition of instruction'—the precise lens or scenario you must adopt to evaluate the statements—which is essential for data validity.

step_presort.what: Provide background information to help characterize your participant profile.
step_presort.why: This context allows the research team to analyze how distinct viewpoints (factors) may or may not correlate with specific demographic variables or professional backgrounds within the participant group.

step_rough.what: Sort the deck of statements into three initial piles based on your immediate reaction.
step_rough.why: This preparatory step is designed to reduce cognitive load; it allows you to familiarize yourself with the full range of the topic (the Q-set) before the more demanding task of comparative ranking begins.

step_fine.what: Place the statements onto the grid structure, moving from the extremes toward the center.
step_fine.why: The grid's structure imposes a 'forced distribution' that prevents you from rating everything as equally important; it compels you to make trade-offs, thereby modelling the relative significance of each idea in your specific belief system.

step_post.what: Confirm your grid arrangement and add comments explaining your strongest choices.
step_post.why: Your qualitative explanations are critical for validating the statistical analysis; they help the researchers interpret the data through your logic rather than imposing their own assumptions on your choices.

consent.label: I confirm that i have read the above information and consent to the processing of my data for the purposes of this study.
consent.error: Please accept the terms to continue.

welcome.reset_confirm: Are you sure you want to start a new session? This will clear your current progress.
welcome.default_instructions:
1. **Let's meet:** First, you will be asked a few short, preliminary questions.
2. **First impressions:** You will read through the different statements and roughly sort them according to your personal viewpoint: whether you tend to agree, feel neutral, or disagree with them.
3. **Your perspective:** During the sorting stage, you will place the statements on a grid to refine your viewpoint. You will sort and rank the statements from “most agree” to “most disagree” by placing each statement in the corresponding box.
4. **Why:** Before the session ends, you will be asked a few clarifying questions to help us better understand your most significant choices.

profile.title: Let's meet
profile.description: First, you will be asked a few short, preliminary questions.

rough.title: First impressions
rough.description: You will read through the different statements and roughly sort them according to your personal viewpoint: whether you tend to agree, feel neutral, or disagree with them. It will make the next task easier.

fine.title: Your perspective
fine.description: During the sorting stage, you will place the statements on a grid to refine your viewpoint. You will sort and rank the statements from “most agree” to “most disagree” by placing each statement in the corresponding box. There are no right or wrong answers. The best answer is the one that reflects your opinion, or beliefs.

post.title: Why
post.description: Before the session ends, you will be asked a few clarifying questions to help us better understand your most significant choices, as well as a few questions about your background.

consent.title: Informed consent and data processing agreement
consent.subtitle: Please read the following information carefully.
consent.default_text:
1. Nature of the study and participant understanding. You understand that this exercise employs q-methodology, a research technique designed to model subjective viewpoints. The task requires you to sort statements according to their significance relative to your personal perspective; consequently, there are no objective "right" or "wrong" answers.

2. Data processing and pseudonymization (GDPR compliance).

De-identification and retention:. This technical process ensures that your digital footprint cannot be used to re-identify you once the session ends.

Exception for follow-up: if you voluntarily provide your contact details at the end of the study for a follow-up contact, the link between your identity (email) and your response will be maintained strictly for the duration of that specific follow-up phase.

Reporting: the final results will be presented in an anonymized format. Data will be aggregated to reveal shared social perspectives (factors). Qualitative comments may be quoted to contextualize these factors but will be screened to remove revealing details.

3. Voluntary participation and right to withdraw. You maintain the right to discontinue the sorting process and close your browser at any time prior to submission without penalty.

Pre-submission: if you withdraw before finalizing your sort, no partial data will be retained.

Post-submission: due to the statistical nature of q-methodology (factor analysis), once your data has been submitted and aggregated into the dataset, individual withdrawal may not be feasible.

4. Data retention and rights

presort.title: Before we start
presort.description: Please answer a few questions to help us situate your viewpoint.

header.subtitle: Swipe **left** to disagree, **right** to agree, or **down** to be neutral.
header.hint: This is spontaneous — you can change your mind right after.

complete.title: First step complete
complete.subtitle: We will now refine the nuances.

instructions.desktop_tip: Drag, press, or use keyboard ( ← ↓ → ) to sort

deck.title: Available statements
deck.all_placed: All statements in this pile are sorted

workbench.drag_or_tap: Drag to grid or tap slot

methodology.extremes: Tip: start with what is most obvious to you (the extremes).
methodology.vertical: In the same column, the order from top to bottom doesn't matter.
methodology.constraint: You have to fit everything! It can be a puzzle, but that's how we prioritize choices.
methodology.relative: Reminder: items are sorted relative to each other (relative agreement).
methodology.zoom: Feel free to zoom in on different parts of the grid for better visibility.
methodology.flexibility: Nothing is final: you can move cards or put them back in the deck at any time.

post.title: Your perspective & feedback
post.description: You're almost done! Your comments on the most significant statements are crucial for our analysis.
post.step2_description: Please answer these final questions to complete the study.

extreme.title: Key Choices
extreme.why: Why did you choose to place this statement here? How is it significant to you?
extreme.placeholder: E.g., a specific event, a strong belief, or a particular word in the statement...
extreme.min_chars: A few words are enough to help us understand the context.

optional.title: Did any statements feel particularly surprising, unclear, or confusing to you? If so, why?
optional.description: You are also welcome to add comments to any other statement if you would like to further explain your choices.

post.validation_error: Please fill in all required fields.

success.title: Thank you for your participation!
success.message: Your contribution to the study has been saved.

contact.title: Results & contact
contact.interview_consent: I agree to be contacted by the researcher for follow-up.
contact.newsletter_consent: I wish to be informed about the study results.
contact.gdpr_note: Your email will never be publicly shared. Providing it ensures pseudonymization: your data remains identifiable only for research purposes by the investigator.
contact.pseudonymization_note: Agreeing to be contacted implies that the researcher can link your perspective to your email (pseudonymization) for this specific follow-up.
contact.error_invalid_email: Please enter a valid email address

intro.consent_desc: Participants must agree to these terms before starting.

condition.title: Condition of instruction
condition.label: Define condition of instruction
condition.desc: This is the core prompt that guides participants throughout the sorting process.
condition.placeholder: E.g. Please rank the following statements from those you most agree with to those you most disagree with
condition.grid_desc: This is the core instruction guiding participants during the grid sorting process.
condition.pre_desc: Instruction given to participants during the initial rough sort.
condition.pre_placeholder: E.g. Based on your personal point of view; divide the cards into three piles...

tips.title: Why is this important?
tips.desc: In q-methodology, the 'condition of instruction' determines the point of view from which the participant sorts the items. It should be clear, singular, and consistent across all sorting phases.

questions.enable_presort_desc: Collect demographic information or other data before the sorting task.

empty.title: No questions yet
empty.desc: Click above to add your first question

bulk.title: Bulk editor (quick paste)

set.title: Q-set
set.confirm_clear: Are you sure you want to delete ALL statements? This cannot be undone.

settings.title: Research settings
settings.desc: Configure how statements are presented to participants
settings.show_codes_desc: Display statement codes (e.g., "S1", "S2") alongside the text.
settings.randomize_desc: Present statements in random order to each participant (consistent within session)

grid.title: Forced distribution grid
grid.desc: The grid shape forces participants to discriminate between statements, approximating a normal distribution.
grid.symmetry_lock_desc: Automatically apply changes to opposite columns to maintain a balanced distribution.
grid.auto_balance_desc: Reshape grid to a balanced semi-normal distribution
grid.reshaped: Grid reshaped to a balanced distribution
grid.no_statements: Add statements first to auto-shape the grid
grid.tooltip_desc: Q-methodology uses a quasi-normal distribution (kurtosis > 0) to ensure participants make meaningful distinctions. The pyramid shape prevents central tendency bias.
grid.locked_desc: Structural changes (statement codes, grid) are disabled because the study has collected data or is no longer in draft mode. You can still edit text translations.
grid.locked_active_desc: The study is currently collecting data. To modify the design, you must first revert it to draft mode (warning: this may invalidate existing results).
grid.locked_paused_desc: The study is currently paused. Design remains locked to protect existing data.
grid.locked_closed_desc: This study is completed. The design is archived and cannot be changed.
grid.mismatch_desc: You have {{statements}} statements but the grid only has slots for {{slots}} items. Please adjust the columns below to match.
grid.unlock_hint: Go to data explorer to purge sessions and unlock structure

extreme.desc: Select columns that trigger follow-up questions.
extreme.prompt_placeholder: Why did you place this statement here?

random_comments.title: Allow random comments
random_comments.desc: Allow participants to add comments to any statement in the grid.

custom.desc: Add custom questions to the post-sort survey.
